meta-runner.name: Web Deploy
meta-runner.description: Step for explicit use of msdeploy to deploy the output of dotnet publish

param.name: webdeploy.sourcePath
param.spec: text label='Source path' description='Path to published output. Default: %teamcity.build.checkoutDir%\publish'

param.name: webdeploy.siteName
param.spec: text label='IIS site name' description='Example: MySite or Default Web Site/MyApp'

param.name: webdeploy.password
param.spec: text label='Password' description='Use secret parameter for password. Example: %PublishPassword%'

param.name: webdeploy.skipAppData
param.spec: checkbox label='Skip App_Data' description='If enabled, App_Data folder (and its content) on destination will not be deleted during deploy (same behavior as .NET Framework WebDeploy)' checkedValue='true' uncheckedValue='false'

param.name: webdeploy.setAppDataPermissions
param.spec: checkbox label='Set App_Data permissions' description='If enabled, sets write permissions for the IIS application pool identity on the App_Data folder.' checkedValue='true' uncheckedValue='false'

parameters.param:
where msdeploy.exe if errorlevel 1 exit /b 1
set SKIP_APP_DATA= if "%webdeploy.skipAppData%"=="true" set SKIP_APP_DATA=-skip:skipAction=Delete,objectName=dirPath,absolutePath=App_Data -skip:skipAction=Delete,objectName=filePath,absolutePath=App_Data
msdeploy.exe ^ -verb:sync ^ -source:contentPath="%webdeploy.sourcePath%" ^ -dest:contentPath="%webdeploy.siteName%",computerName="%webdeploy.url%?site=%webdeploy.siteName%",userName="%webdeploy.username%",password="%webdeploy.password%",authType="Basic",includeAcls="False" ^ -enableRule:AppOffline ^ -allowUntrusted ^ %SKIP_APP_DATA% ^ -verbose if errorlevel 1 exit /b 1
if "%webdeploy.setAppDataPermissions%"=="true" ( msdeploy.exe ^ -verb:sync ^ -source:setAcl ^ -dest:setAcl="%webdeploy.siteName%/App_Data",setAclAccess=Write,computerName="%webdeploy.url%?site=%webdeploy.siteName%",userName="%webdeploy.username%",password="%webdeploy.password%",authType="Basic" ^ -allowUntrusted ^ -verbose if errorlevel 1 exit /b 1 )
